words.oneLiner: Transcending established national boundaries or spheres of interest.
words.example: Supranational courts can overrule some national statutes.

words.oneLiner: Without qualification.
words.example: A thoroughgoing reform replaced outdated procedures entirely.

words.oneLiner: The violation of a law or a duty or moral principle.
words.example: Forgiveness followed sincere apology for the transgression.

words.oneLiner: Not having or showing an enlightened outlook.
words.example: Unenlightened policies kept girls out of school for years.

words.oneLiner: Having no precedent.
words.example: The storm caused unprecedented damage along the coast.

words.oneLiner: The act of throwing someone or something out of a window.
words.example: The second defenestration of Prague ignited wider conflict.

words.oneLiner: The quality of being discursive.
words.example: Discursiveness in the essay wandered far from its thesis.

words.oneLiner: Deprive of voting rights.
words.example: Laws that disenfranchise voters face swift legal challenge.

words.oneLiner: With little or no preparation or forethought.
words.example: Her extemporaneous remarks moved the room more than slides.

words.oneLiner: Intellectuals or highly educated people as a group, especially when regarded.
words.example: The intelligentsia debated reform in smoky cafés.

words.oneLiner: The branch of economics that studies the overall working of a national economy.
words.example: Macroeconomics tracks inflation, unemployment, and growth.

words.oneLiner: Economics focused on households and individual firms.
words.example: Microeconomics explains how firms set prices and wages.

words.oneLiner: Given to using long words; a very long word itself.
words.example: His sesquipedalian vocabulary intimidated first-year students.

words.oneLiner: How easily someone or something can be affected.
words.example: Genetic susceptibility can raise risk without guaranteeing disease.

words.oneLiner: Worn to shreds.
words.example: A tatterdemalion coat hung on the scarecrow by the field.

words.oneLiner: The act of abandoning a party for cause.
words.example: Years of tergiversation ruined his reputation for honesty.

words.oneLiner: Not making concessions.

words.oneLiner: Doctrine that the useful is the good.
words.example: Utilitarianism judges actions by the happiness they produce.

words.oneLiner: Of or relating to or characteristic of a person who is a valetudinarian.
words.example: A valetudinarian cousin canceled trips over minor colds.

words.oneLiner: The appearance of truth.
words.example: The novel's verisimilitude made fictional towns feel real.

words.oneLiner: Third from last.
words.example: The antepenultimate syllable carries stress in that word.

words.oneLiner: A diplomat who is fully authorized to represent his or her government.
words.example: A plenipotentiary signed the treaty on behalf of the crown.

words.oneLiner: Not particularly attractive or appealing to the eye.
words.example: An unprepossessing shopfront hid an excellent bakery inside.

words.oneLiner: The representation of objects (especially a god) as having human form or traits.
words.example: Anthropomorphism in cartoons gives animals human speech.

words.oneLiner: Not able to be denied or disputed.
words.example: Video evidence provided incontrovertible proof of theft.

words.oneLiner: The sound of a bell ringing.
words.example: Tintinnabulation from church bells marked noon in the village.
